title: Microsoft Announces Major Changes to Xbox Gaming Division
subtitle: The technology giant plans to cut thousands of jobs and change how it manages its game studios to improve financial results.

summary.en: Microsoft is making significant changes to its Xbox gaming business this week. The company announced plans to lay off over 3,000 employees within the division. These moves follow earlier reports of even larger job cuts across the global company. Microsoft aims to focus its resources on its most successful projects. The restructuring also includes selling or closing several smaller game studios.
summary.zh: 微軟本週正針對其 Xbox 遊戲業務進行重大調整。公司宣布計畫在該部門裁減超過三千名員工。這些舉措是在先前關於全球公司更大規模裁員的報導之後所做出的。微軟旨在將資源集中於其最成功的專案上。此次重組也包含出售或關閉數間較小的遊戲工作室。

paragraphs.en: Microsoft is making major changes to its Xbox gaming division this week as part of a plan to improve the company's financial health. According to NPR Business, the technology giant has announced that it will lay off over 3,000 staff members. This decision is part of a larger effort to reorganize the business and focus on the most important projects.
paragraphs.zh: 微軟 (Microsoft) 本週正針對其 Xbox 遊戲部門進行重大調整，作為改善公司財務狀況計畫的一部分。根據美國國家公共廣播電台 (NPR) 商業報導，這家科技巨擘已宣布將裁減超過三千名員工。此項決策是公司重組業務並聚焦於最重要專案之廣泛努力的一環。

paragraphs.en: These job cuts are the latest in a series of difficult announcements for the company. Earlier reports from this week showed that Microsoft had planned to reduce its global workforce by 4,800 people, which is about 2% of all its employees. A large portion of these job losses is happening specifically within the Xbox gaming group. This "hard reset" — a term used to describe a complete restart or major change in direction — shows that the gaming industry is currently facing serious challenges.
paragraphs.zh: 這些裁員是該公司近期一系列艱難公告中的最新消息。本週稍早的報導顯示，微軟原先計畫在全球裁減四千八百名員工，約佔其總員工數的百分之二。這些裁員中有很大一部分集中在 Xbox 遊戲部門。這種「徹底重整」——用來描述完全重新開始或重大方向轉變的術語——顯示遊戲產業目前正面臨嚴峻挑戰。

paragraphs.en: As part of this new strategy, Microsoft is also changing how it manages its game studios. The company plans to close or sell four of its studios. Some of these studios will now operate independently, meaning they will no longer be owned or managed by Microsoft. By doing this, the company hopes to save money and move its resources toward high-priority projects. One example of a project that will receive more attention is the popular Fallout series.
paragraphs.zh: 作為這項新策略的一部分，微軟也正在改變其管理遊戲工作室的方式。公司計畫關閉或出售旗下四間工作室。其中部分工作室將轉為獨立運作，意即不再由微軟擁有或管理。透過此舉，公司希望能節省開支，並將資源轉向高優先級的專案。將獲得更多關注的專案之一，便是熱門的《異塵餘生》(Fallout) 系列。

paragraphs.en: Industry experts are watching these events closely to understand what they mean for the future of gaming. The gaming market has grown very quickly in recent years, but many companies are now finding it harder to make a profit. Microsoft’s decision to cut staff and sell studios reflects a broader trend where big companies are becoming more careful with their spending. Investors often look for signs that a company is becoming more efficient, but these changes are very difficult for the workers who are losing their jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 產業專家正密切關注這些事件，以了解其對遊戲產業未來的意義。遊戲市場近年來成長迅速，但許多公司現在發現獲利變得更加困難。微軟裁員與出售工作室的決定，反映出大型企業在支出上變得更加謹慎的廣泛趨勢。投資人通常會尋找公司正變得更有效率的跡象，但這些變動對於失去工作的員工而言卻是非常艱難的。

paragraphs.en: For the employees at Xbox, this is a period of great uncertainty. Layoffs — which occur when a company ends an employee's contract for business reasons rather than because of poor performance — are always difficult for a company's culture. While Microsoft has not shared every detail about which specific teams will be affected, the scale of the cuts suggests that almost every part of the gaming division will feel the impact. The company is trying to balance the need for better financial results with the need to keep creating high-quality games that players want to buy.
paragraphs.zh: 對於 Xbox 的員工來說，這是一個充滿高度不確定性的時期。裁員——即公司因商業理由而非員工表現不佳而終止合約——對企業文化而言總是困難的。雖然微軟尚未分享關於哪些特定團隊會受影響的完整細節，但裁員規模顯示遊戲部門的幾乎每個環節都會感受到衝擊。公司正試圖在追求更好的財務表現，與持續創造玩家願意購買的高品質遊戲之間取得平衡。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the success of this plan will depend on whether Microsoft can keep its most important games on track while managing a much smaller team. The decision to focus on big, successful brands like Fallout suggests that the company wants to play it safe. By putting its money and time into projects that already have many fans, Microsoft hopes to ensure that its gaming business remains profitable in the long term. However, some critics worry that by closing smaller studios, the company might lose the creativity that helps new and interesting games reach the market.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，這項計畫的成功將取決於微軟能否在管理規模縮減的團隊同時，確保其最重要的遊戲專案進度順利。決定聚焦於像《異塵餘生》這樣大型且成功的品牌，顯示公司希望採取穩健策略。透過將資金與時間投入已有廣大粉絲基礎的專案，微軟希望確保其遊戲業務能長期獲利。然而，部分評論家擔心，透過關閉較小的工作室，公司可能會失去協助創新且有趣的遊戲進入市場的創造力。

paragraphs.en: For now, the gaming community is waiting to see how these changes will affect upcoming game releases. Microsoft has not yet provided a full timeline for when all the studio sales or closures will be finished. As the company moves forward with this restructuring, the focus will likely remain on its financial performance and its ability to compete with other major players in the gaming industry. The coming months will be important as the company tries to stabilize its business and show that its new strategy can work in a very competitive market.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，遊戲社群正等待觀察這些變動將如何影響即將推出的遊戲。微軟尚未提供所有工作室出售或關閉完成的完整時間表。隨著公司持續進行重組，焦點可能會持續放在其財務表現，以及與遊戲產業其他主要競爭對手抗衡的能力上。未來幾個月將至關重要，因為公司正試圖穩定業務，並證明其新策略能在競爭激烈的市場中奏效。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A complete restart or a major change in direction.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 重新設定；徹底的改變或重啟。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（重置），這裡作名詞用。文中：This "hard reset" — a term used to describe a complete restart or major change in direction — shows that the gaming industry is currently facing serious challenges.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To choose a course of action that involves little or no risk.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個慣用語，字面意思與實際意義不同，指選擇風險較小的做法。文中：The decision to focus on big, successful brands like Fallout suggests that the company wants to play it safe.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Making progress as planned or expected.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述火車軌道，這裡引申為計畫進度正常。文中：Looking ahead, the success of this plan will depend on whether Microsoft can keep its most important games on track while managing a much smaller team.

sources.name: NPR Business
sources.title: Microsoft is doing a hard reset of its XBOX gaming division

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Makes Major Changes to Xbox Business
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft is undergoing a significant restructuring of its Xbox division to improve financial health. The company plans to lay off 3,200 employees and close or sell four studios. Resources are being moved toward high-priority projects like the Fallout series. Industry experts suggest these changes reflect a broader shift in Microsoft's strategy. The future of the Xbox brand remains a subject of intense debate among market analysts.

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Announces Major Job Cuts and Xbox Restructuring
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft has announced that it will cut 4,800 jobs, representing about 2% of its global workforce. A large portion of these layoffs will occur within the Xbox gaming division. The company is spinning off four game studios to operate independently. This decision is part of a larger strategy to focus on artificial intelligence and high-profit gaming projects. Leadership stated that these changes are necessary to keep the company competitive in a fast-changing industry.

previousArticles.title: Microsoft Makes Major Changes to Xbox Business Strategy
previousArticles.summaryEn: Microsoft is undergoing a massive restructuring of its Xbox division to improve its financial health. The company plans to lay off 3,200 employees and close or sell several studios. Resources are being moved toward high-priority projects like the popular Fallout series. Experts are now questioning the long-term future of the Xbox brand within Microsoft. The company aims to focus on its most successful games while managing rising costs.

quiz.question: What is the total number of job cuts planned for the Xbox gaming group specifically, according to the article?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，Microsoft 宣佈在 Xbox 部門裁員超過 3,000 人。選項 A 與 C 提到的 4,800 人及 2% 是指 Microsoft 全球範圍的總裁員數據，而非僅針對 Xbox 部門。選項 D 提到四個工作室，但文章並未指明這四個工作室的具體員工總數，因此無法選出。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about Microsoft's future strategy for game development?

options.A: They will focus exclusively on creating new, experimental game titles.
options.B: They intend to expand their studio count to increase production speed.
options.C: They are prioritizing established, popular franchises to reduce financial risk.
options.D: They plan to stop all game production to focus on other business sectors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段提到 Microsoft 決定將資源集中在像 Fallout 這樣的大型成功品牌上，並使用「play it safe」來描述此策略，顯示其目標是確保長期獲利。選項 A 與文章內容相反，因為他們正在減少對小型工作室的投入；選項 B 與文中「close or sell four studios」的縮減策略矛盾；選項 D 錯誤，因為公司仍致力於維持遊戲業務的競爭力。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does the phrase 'hard reset' mean in the context of Microsoft's business changes?

options.A: A physical repair of broken gaming hardware.
options.B: A complete restart or a significant shift in direction.
options.C: A temporary pause in game development to fix software bugs.
options.D: A difficult challenge that employees must overcome together.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段括號內明確定義了「hard reset」為「a complete restart or major change in direction」，意指公司進行徹底的組織重組。選項 A 誤解了「reset」在商業語境中的比喻意義；選項 C 雖然提到暫停，但未涵蓋「方向改變」的核心意義；選項 D 雖然提到困難，但這不是該術語的定義，而是該行為帶來的影響。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The gaming industry is currently the most profitable sector for technology giants.
options.B: Microsoft is struggling to manage its employees' performance and culture.
options.C: The Fallout series is the only successful game that Microsoft currently owns.
options.D: Microsoft is restructuring its Xbox division to improve financial efficiency in a challenging market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文的核心在於探討 Microsoft 為何進行裁員與工作室調整，即為了提升財務健康並應對競爭激烈的市場。選項 A 與文章提到的「finding it harder to make a profit」矛盾；選項 B 僅是裁員帶來的副作用，而非報導主旨；選項 C 將 Fallout 視為唯一成功遊戲是過度解讀，文章僅將其作為高優先級項目的例子。
